RepealsEffective , the permanent appropriation provided for in former was repealed by , , such act authorizing in lieu thereof, an annual appropriation from the general fund of the Treasury.July 1, 1935section 547 of this titleact June 26, 1934, ch. 756, § 248 Stat. 1226

Redemption of Bonds; Termination of Circulating NotesIn a communication from the Treasury Department dated , it was stated “The Secretary of the Treasury called for redemption the only outstanding issues of United States bonds bearing the circulation privilege as follows:February 17, 1941“2% Consols. of 1930, as of ,July 1, 1935“2% Panama Canal bonds of 1916–36, and“2% Panama Canal bonds of 1918–38, as of .August 1, 1935“The retirement of these issues automatically put an end to National Bank note circulation and the collection of the tax thereon.”
